name: Dieter Rams
description: Less, but better. The ten principles still read like a manifesto.

name: Charles & Ray Eames
description: Plywood, fiberglass, film — a studio that treated everything as material.

name: Verner Panton
description: Colour as structure. The Panton Chair still looks like it arrived from elsewhere.

name: Virgil Abloh
description: The quotation mark as critique. Streetwear elevated to cultural commentary.

name: Jony Ive
description: Aluminium unibody. The iPod wheel. Objects that felt inevitable.

name: Marc Newson
description: Biomorphic forms in aluminium and neoprene — Lockheed Lounge to Apple Watch.

name: Naoto Fukasawa
description: Super Normal. Design that disappears into daily life.

name: Le Corbusier
description: Modulor, concrete, the LC collection — architecture as machine for living.

name: Zaha Hadid
description: Fluid geometry before the software made it easy.

name: Issey Miyake
description: Pleats Please. Fabric engineered like industrial design.

name: James Turrell
description: Light as material. Skyspaces that slow time.

name: Donald Judd
description: Specific objects. Marfa. Permanence in proportion.
